heading: Coordination with health services development activities carried out under the National Health Planning and Resources Development Act of 1974
label: 8156 Coordination with health services development activities carried out under the National Health Planning and Resources Development Act of 1974
text: The Secretary and the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall, to the maximum extent practicable, coordinate programs carried out under this subchapter and programs carried out under part F of title XVI of the Public Health Service Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 300t
notes: References in TextThe National Health Planning and Resources Development Act of 1974, referred to in section catchline, is , , , which is classified generally to subchapters XIII (§ 300k et seq.) and XIV (§ 300 et seq.) of chapter 6A of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title of 1975 Amendment note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 93–64188 Stat. 2225section 201 of Title 42Jan. 4, 1975oThe Public Health Service Act, referred to in text, is , . Part F of title XVI of the Public Health Service Act was added by act , , , and was redesignated as Part D by , , . Part D of that Act is classified generally to Part D (§ 300t) of subchapter XIV of chapter 6A of Title 42. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.act July 1, 1944, ch. 37358 Stat. 682Pub. L. 93–641, § 488 Stat. 2273Pub. Amendments1991— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–40section 5056 of this titlesubstituted “The Secretary” for “The Administrator”.Pub. L. 102–831982—, substituted “Health and Human Services” for “Health, Education, and Welfare”.Pub. L. 97–295, § 4(95)(A), inserted “( et seq.)” after “part F of title XVI of the Public Health Service Act”.Pub. L. 97–295, § 4(93)42 U.S.C. 300t1976— substituted “health services development activities carried out under the National Health Planning and Resources Development Act of 1974” for “programs carried out under the Heart Disease, Cancer, and Stroke Amendments of 1965” in section catchline and “part F of title XVI” for “title IX” in text.Pub.
